body.content:
TheOnlyJJ: "How do you enjoy being an Official Factions Hub Server?"

AJ: "I'd say that it's quite a good feeling just having your own faction recognised by the developers and just the people that make SCP:RP be what it actually is. I think it's quite an amazing title to have and I know that everyone that has helped me to actually get here is also very proud and is very happy for having the title." 

TheOnlyJJ: "Tell me about yourself, what is your position within the server."

AJ:" Well, I am AJ, the Command of Omega-1 "Law's left Hand" that quite recently got promoted out of juniors and into officially verified within the Faction Hub which literally everyone I know is happy for me and my faction and the people within my faction are quite overjoyed."

TheOnlyJJ: "What’s your background with the franchise"

AJ: "Well most of my life, I truly do believe that there may be anomalous things within the world and from quite a young age, have quite enjoyed it a lot. My friend back in 2020 did show me SCP:RP when it was recently made and I will admit, I did find it quite fun but then with covid and everything, I did stop playing and did entirely forget about the game. However then in November of 2024, I was just looking on Roblox and found SCP:RP and decided to give it another shot and I played a few sites and will admit, I got pretty addicted. I then made Omega-1 "Law's Left Hand" not long after and have tried working on the faction ever since, we have had our ups and down however, we have made it quite the journey and I will probably always love SCP:RP and just the franchise as it is."


TheOnlyJJ: "How do you see your project evolving over the next year?"

AJ: "Personally, I see my faction as quite successful for the ups and downs we have had on our way however, it was only in September/October the High Command team decided to fully focus on the faction which then led us to get SCP:RP Faction Hub verified. I do feel like it will continue to increase with a few projects we do have upcoming and reviving which at the moment do not seem to be going successful however, I feel like if we keep going, we will have it successful and achieve our goals."

Omega-1 "Law’s Left Hand" is now officially a Verified Faction. This group is an excellent option for players interested in their specific roleplay style and gameplay mechanics. Currently boasting over 200 members, Omega-1 is rapidly becoming a prominent force within the SCP Roleplay Factions Hub.
The owner, AJ, celebrated the achievement of verified status following the faction's consistent performance. With numerous successful deployments—primarily centered at Area-14—Omega-1 is establishing itself as a formidable Mobile Task Force within custom servers. We encourage all verified sites to engage with other groups in the Factions Hub to further expand their reach and collaborative opportunities.
Roblox Chat Sanctions: How Badly Were We Affected?

This was the date where Roblox imposed worldwide chat restrictions, taking the entire community by surprise. To games with chat at the core of their gameplay, like SCP: Roleplay, this could very well end the RP genre.
Concerned members of the community voiced these thoughts, with Metamethod giving his own statement and thoughts concerning the problem.

body.alt: Statement by Metamethod, addressing concerns regarding the future of the game

body.content: Yet, despite these challenges, the community of SCP:RP has overcome all odds, and sites still thrive to this day. The Faction Hub News Team has invited the owners of sites across SCP:RP to share their inspiring testimonies.

"Well, I believe that we as a community took the punch really well. At first, it did bother us a bit more than it should, but with time it all got better. After maybe a month, we got used to chat restrictions and simply not being able to talk to some people, but it is what it is."

"The hardest part about it all that I personally noticed is not being able to PM, alert, or notify people about events via announcements, so we started using subtitles and titles to communicate with people (somewhat like a site wide broadcast). We also use regular announcements due to them showing up on TVs. At this moment, we have actually adapted fully to the changes that Roblox forcibly gave us, and we are still pushing through it even though it's quite sad due to us leaning (at this moment) more towards the serious roleplay side with our latest roleplay additions."

"Now, activity. I personally did see a bit of a decrease in players, but nothing drastic. It really all comes down to time zones and people being busy with life, but we are still pulling some good numbers, and I’m really satisfied with community and staff activity as of now. I love my ARA-14 goons a lot! Truly the best community around here, not glazing son. . ."

"Since the Roblox update regarding chat restrictions, our site has struggled to keep an active and engaging player base. With the community being unable to communicate with other age groups, this severely limits roleplay, which is the biggest aspect of Site-64."

 "We had just reached 10,000 members within our community before the update rolled out globally, and since the update released, that number had dropped significantly. From the day the update was released, to today (09/04/26), we have lost 800 members. I think it’s safe to say that the player base we once had has completely given up on the roleplay aspect of our site."

"Unfortunately, not much can be done about this change, as any methods which attempt to bypass these restrictions is strictly against Roblox’s Terms of Service, however the Administration Team at Site-64 is hard at work to find possible solutions to overcome these restrictions. Our Roleplay Lobby is working effortlessly to create a designated roleplay server on Discord, allowing our community to roleplay as their characters freely, all from Discord."

"I think we all hoped that Roblox would listen to the community and their concerns, as well as their own stocks dropping drastically after the update was pushed out, but it seems like Roblox isn’t budging on this. Site-64’s activity has dropped severely because of this update, and we have lost too many members as a result. It’s truly a shame to see this happening, and I’m sure we are not the only roleplay group who is being affected in similar ways in other Roblox games."

"The age restrictions have impacted both the ability of players to roleplay with a wide range of other users, whilst also impacting the ability of our moderation staff to ensure that chat behaviour is kept appropriate."

"One of the ways that these impacts are mitigated is a number of factions introducing age bracket roles within their communications server to be able to highlight individuals who will be able to communicate to one another, this has been particularly useful for factions such as L-88 (our primary checkpoint guarding unit) where being able to communicate with one another is vital. This has also been used to host age bracketed tryouts, as again being able to communicate with attendees is crucial. "

"Additionally, a number of our staff have implemented their age bracket as part of their on-duty role tags so that users can identify moderators that specific individuals will be able to report issues to based on their age bracket. Of course, this is less of an issue as we have our communication server, and namely our ticket system, where reports can be made more easily."

"We have also fairly recently introduced several roleplay-centric channels to allow users that would not otherwise be able to communicate to do so, primarily focused on our various checkpoints within the map."

Truly, these statements show the optimism and adaptability of this community. The above three statements have demonstrated the flexibility and open-mindedness of the leaders within the sites, while showing the dedication of our community through this period of uncertainty.

Would SCP:RP face more challenges? Based on current affairs, chances are yes. As Roblox continues to evolve, overcoming them will be all the more harder, and we would need to adapt to them better.

But we will.
